title: schima wallichii subsp. crenata
intro: Schima wallichii subsp. crenata, also known as the "Rays of the Sun" or "Diamond Flower", is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Theaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant's leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Schima wallichii subsp. crenata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and used in capsule or powder form to harness its medicinal benefits.
